body_txt: This is a specific version of Living a Double Life in which that second life is being a sex worker of some kind: stripper, prostitute, High-Class Call Girl , cam girl, phone sex operator, rental Dominatrix , Compensated Dating , etc. This character is primarily concerned with keeping their two lives separate, or at the very least, keeping their sex work from bleeding into their non-sex work activities. They might have a more typical 9-to-5 job that they work most days and engage in sex work for the "nightshift." Or sex work is their primary job, however, they try to keep that information from those in their life that are unaffiliated with the industry. If troubles arise—and they always will—it'll be because this character can no longer keep their two lives separated. Often, the issue will be a character who knows them in their "daytime" mode finding out about their "nighttime" activities and judging or criticizing them for it. Sometimes this will go so far as publicly outing them and opening them up to public ridicule and Slut-Shaming . Frequently the character who finds out is a Love Interest , adding romantic rejection to the betrayal. Another common problem is when the seedier aspects of being a sex worker encroach on and threaten this character's public lifestyle and image: Predatory Pimps , obsessive clients, jealous and spiteful rival sex workers , and suspicious police officers may harass them even when they aren't on the clock. They might have to engage in illegal Shady Lady of the Night -related activities even when they aren't officially working. And pictures, videos, and other recordings of them working can get leaked, jeopardizing their non-sex work careers. Note, what's important to this trope is that the character must be actively trying to hide the fact that they are a sex worker from others. A character simply not knowing someone else is a sex worker and finding out about it later doesn't count unless the character was trying to conceal that information. Similarly, a character being a sex worker as a side gig doesn't automatically count if they're not trying to hide it from others. Sub-Trope of Living a Double Life . May overlap with Seemingly Wholesome '50s Girl and Naughty by Night . Compare Single Mom Stripper and Don't Tell Mama , the former having some examples involve the mom trying to conceal the fact that she strips from her family ( especially her children ) and the latter overlapping if the child is trying to hide their sex work from their parents. Contrast Unproblematic Prostitution , as the job being treated as problematic in-universe is part of the reason why these characters feel the need to hide doing it in the first place. A popular variant for teen dramas focuses on a Troubled Teen or School Idol becoming this trope. See Porn Creator Going Mainstream for cases where sex workers later try to make it in Safe For Work entertainment. Also see Platonic Prostitution , when someone hires a sex worker to do work that isn’t sexual.
